# Dylan McDermott

> Meteorologist and geospatial data professional specializing in weather and catastrophe analysis, geospatial automation, field sensing, remote sensing, and atmospheric research.

## Profile

- Location: West Des Moines, Iowa, USA
- Current role: Meteorologist - Weather and Catastrophe Analyst, Property & Liability Resource Bureau (2025-Present)
- Education: M.S. and B.S. in Meteorology, Iowa State University
- Contact: dylanddermott@gmail.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dylan-mcdermott-193b18174/

## Work Examples

- [PLRB - Meteorologist](https://dylanmcd16.github.io/?work=plrb-weather-systems): Develops weather-data workflows and geospatial products for event verification, catastrophe analysis, and decision support. Work includes meteorological interpretation, Python and ArcPy automation, ArcGIS applications, cloud services, APIs, and clear communication of uncertainty.
- [Corteva - Research Associate](https://dylanmcd16.github.io/?work=corteva-field-sensing): Built field-sensing systems and automated data pipelines that converted raw observations into quality-controlled geospatial analyses. Work includes Smartstick field collection, cloud and ArcGIS workflows, drone imagery, LiDAR, a Gold Standard weather station, soil-gas experiments, and infrared-radiometer fleet operations.
- [Master's Thesis - Iowa State University](https://dylanmcd16.github.io/?work=land-use-convective-weather): Studied how historical land-use change affected Midwest rainfall and mesoscale convective systems using WRF and Noah-MP simulations.

## Core Capabilities

Meteorological analysis, catastrophe research, spatial analysis, Python automation, ArcPy, ArcGIS Enterprise, data integration, REST APIs, radar, numerical weather prediction, remote sensing, field sensing, drone imagery, LiDAR, WRF, Noah-MP, AWS, SQL, PostgreSQL, React, TypeScript, FastAPI, Git, GitHub, and Linux.
